summaries.title: Effects of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child IQ at 4 and 8 years in a UK birth cohort study.
summaries.summary: A UK birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal lead (Pb) exposure and child IQ at ages 4 and 8 years.
key_findings: No significant association between prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at 4 or 8 years in adjusted models.
Positive association for IQ at age 8 in girls per 1 mg/dl of prenatal Pb.
Negative coefficients for IQ at age 8 in boys, suggesting boys may be more susceptible to prenatal lead exposure.
captured_features: Child IQ scores (WPPSI at 4 years, WISC-III at 8 years)
summaries.population: Children in the UK (ALSPAC cohort)
limitations: Small sample size for offspring at 30 months (n=235) for certain analyses
Inconsistent findings in previous literature
summaries.data_availability_statement: Taylor, C.M., Kordas, K., Golding, J., Emond, A.M., 2017. Data relating to prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at 4 and 8 years old in the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children doi:http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.neuro.2017.07.025 Data in Brief.

summaries.title: Reduction of in utero lead exposures in South African populations: Positive impact of unleaded petrol.
summaries.summary: The study assessed in utero lead (Pb) exposure and birth outcomes in a South African population following the removal of lead from petrol.
key_findings: Geometric mean of Pb in maternal blood was 1.32 μg/dL
Positive correlation between maternal blood Pb and cord blood Pb (rho = 0.65)
Positive association between cord blood Pb and head circumference in female neonates
Maternal Pb levels correlated with race, education, water sources, cooking fuels, and pesticide use
summaries.population: Pregnant women and neonates in South Africa
summaries.exposure_domain: Lead (Pb) from petrol, paint, and environmental sources
limitations: Focuses on in utero/neonatal outcomes rather than long-term childhood development

summaries.title: The Hokkaido Birth Cohort Study on Environment and Children's Health: cohort profile-updated 2017.
summaries.summary: A profile and update of the Hokkaido Study on Environment and Children’s Health, a prospective birth cohort study investigating the effects of low-level environmental chemical exposures on birth outcomes and child development.
key_findings: Environmental chemical exposures affect genetically susceptible populations and DNA methylation.
Study tracks various chemicals including dioxins, PCBs, organochlorine pesticides, PFAS, phthalates, bisphenol A, and methylmercury.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and children in Japan (Sapporo cohort: 514; Hokkaido cohort: 20,926)
summaries.exposure_domain: environmental chemicals (dioxins, PCBs, pesticides, PFAS, phthalates, bisphenol A, methylmercury, tobacco smoking)
limitations: Ongoing study (report is a profile/update)

summaries.title: Body Burden of Dichlorodiphenyl Dichloroethene (DDE) and Childhood Pulmonary Function.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study investigating the relationship between blood DDE levels and pulmonary function (FVC and FEV1) in children, specifically examining the mediating role of height and weight.
key_findings: DDE measured at eight years had significant, inverse, indirect, and total effects on FVC and FEV1 at ten years.
The effects of DDE on pulmonary function were mediated through its effects on height and weight.
captured_features: Forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV1)
summaries.population: Children aged 8-10 years in central Germany
limitations: Small sample size (n=328)
Requires further testing in regions with ongoing high DDT application

summaries.title: Proteomic Biomarkers for Bisphenol A-Early Exposure and Women's Thyroid Cancer.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the effects of early-life Bisphenol A (BPA) exposure on thyroid tissues using a mouse model and validating findings in human thyroid cancer patients.
key_findings: Identified 17 BPA-responsive proteins in mouse thyroid tissues
Identified nine specific proteins (including Anxa6, Atp5b, Hspa5, and Vcp) as significant
Observed positive association between blood BPA levels and mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP in human thyroid cancer tissues
summaries.study_design: Experimental animal study (mice) and clinical validation (human patients)
summaries.population: Female ICR mice and 28 women with thyroid cancer
limitations: Small human sample size (n=28)
Animal model extrapolation

summaries.title: Assessment of Nutritional Status of Infants Living in Arsenic-Contaminated Areas in Bangladesh and Its Association with Arsenic Exposure.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study assessing the nutritional status of 120 infants (birth to 9 months) in arsenic-contaminated areas of Bangladesh to determine the association between household drinking water arsenic levels and growth indices.
key_findings: Significant association of stunting with household drinking water arsenic exposure ≥50 μg/L at 9 months of age (p = 0.009).
No significant changes found in other nutritional indices (WAZ, WHZ) over time or with arsenic exposure levels.
Stunting rates were 10% at 3 months and 44% at 6 and 9 months.
summaries.population: 120 infants in arsenic-contaminated areas in Bangladesh
limitations: small sample size
short follow-up
potential statistical incongruity regarding stunting at 9 months

summaries.title: Correction to: Early-life exposure to PM2.5 and risk of acute asthma clinical encounters among children in Massachusetts: a case-crossover analysis.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study analyzes 'acute asthma clinical encounters' and refers to a case-crossover analysis of clinical data.
summaries.summary: A case-crossover analysis investigating the association between early-life PM2.5 exposure and acute asthma clinical encounters in children in Massachusetts.
key_findings: Association between PM2.5 exposure and acute asthma clinical encounters (specific results not detailed in correction text).
summaries.population: children in Massachusetts
limitations: Correction only provides demographic data update; full study limitations not in text

summaries.title: Low-fidelity Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus polymerase mutants to improve live-attenuated vaccine safety and efficacy.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of low-fidelity mutations in the RNA-dependent RNA-polymerase (RdRp) of the Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus (VEEV) vaccine strain TC-83 to improve vaccine safety and efficacy.
key_findings: Mutants TC-83 3x and TC-83 4x showed increased immunogenicity and complete protection in adult mice.
Mutants exhibited increased but inconsistent attenuation in infant mice.
Serial passaging in a permissive model led to increased virulence, though they remained less virulent than the parent strain.
Low-fidelity mutations can confer immunogenicity but may lead to vaccine phenotype instability.
summaries.study_design: Experimental laboratory study using murine models and serial passaging
summaries.population: Murine models (infant and adult mice)
limitations: Instability of the vaccine phenotype
Inconsistent attenuation in infant models
summaries.data_availability_statement: Data are not publically available, but will be provided upon request.

summaries.title: Local- and regional-scale air pollution modelling (PM(10)) and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood to age 15 years: Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort, which is a prospective birth cohort study, not a primary EHR or linked health database.
summaries.summary: The study developed and evaluated air pollution modelling (PM10) to estimate exposures during pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood (up to age 15) for the ALSPAC birth cohort.
key_findings: PM10 exposures fell from 31.2 μg/m3 in the first year of life to 19.6 μg/m3 at age ~15.
Largest intra-subject exposure contrasts were between pregnancy trimesters.
Local sources contributed 18.5%–19.5% of PM10 during pregnancy/infancy.
Main roads contributed ~3.0% to total PM10 exposures.
summaries.study_design: Prospective birth cohort exposure assessment using dispersion modelling
summaries.population: ALSPAC birth cohort (children up to age ~15 years, England)
limitations: Lack of fixed-site PM10 monitoring during pregnancy and partial infancy coverage necessitated reliance on dispersion modelling.
summaries.data_availability_statement:
Supplementary material
Supplementary Materials
Image 1

summaries.title: Total mercury exposure in early pregnancy has no adverse association with scholastic ability of the offspring particularly if the mother eats fish.
summaries.summary: Analysis of prenatal total mercury exposure and its association with offspring scholastic abilities (reading, spelling, phoneme awareness, mathematics, and science) using data from the ALSPAC cohort.
key_findings: No evidence of harm associated with total mercury levels provided the mother ate fish during pregnancy.
Math and science skills were unaffected by maternal Hg level if fish was consumed.
No deleterious effects found for reading or spelling results.
captured_features: Child scholastic test scores (reading, spelling, phoneme awareness, mathematics, science)
summaries.population: children in the UK (ALSPAC cohort)
limitations: Small number of participants with specific prenatal mercury and test results (1500 to 2200)
summaries.data_availability_statement: Appendix B.Supplementary data Details of the scholastic tests used.

summaries.title: Occupational exposure to organic solvents during pregnancy and childhood behavior: findings from the PELAGIE birth cohort (France, 2002-2013).
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study investigating the impact of prenatal occupational exposure to organic solvents by mothers on the behavioral traits of their children at ages 2 and 6.
key_findings: Increased externalizing behavior score at age 2 was associated with prenatal solvent exposure.
The association was attenuated at age 6.
No association was observed for internalizing behavior.
summaries.population: Children in the PELAGIE birth cohort (France)
limitations: attenuated effects with aging
potential dilution by postnatal predictors

summaries.title: Analysis By Deep Sequencing of Discontinued Neurotropic Yellow Fever Vaccine Strains.
summaries.summary: Deep sequencing analysis of the discontinued French neurotropic yellow fever (FNV) vaccine strain to characterize its genetic instability and neurotropic properties.
key_findings: FNV strains showed lower polymorphism content than the wild-type parental strain (FVV)
Heterogeneity was observed between different vaccine strains
Reversion to wild-type identity was variably observed
Specific population structures were recovered from vaccines with neurotropic properties
Loss of neurotropism in mice was associated with abundance of wild-type RNA populations
summaries.study_design: In silico deep sequencing and comparative genomic analysis of archive vaccine strains
summaries.population: Archive vaccine strains (French neurotropic vaccine)
limitations: Limited availability of reference material
Inaccessible passage histories
summaries.data_availability_statement: Pre-reduction datasets from sequencing analyses are available from the authors upon request.

summaries.title: Sex-Dependent Impact of Low-Level Lead Exposure during Prenatal Period on Child Psychomotor Functions.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study evaluating the impact of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child psychomotor development and sex-dependent susceptibility.
key_findings: No statistically significant impact of prenatal lead exposure on girls' psychomotor abilities.
Boys showed lower scores for cognitive functions associated with increasing cord blood lead levels (β = −2.07; p = 0.04).
Language and motor abilities were not significantly affected in boys.
Fetal exposure to very low lead levels may affect the early cognitive domain, with boys being more susceptible than girls.
captured_features: Bayley Scales of Infant and Toddler Development scores
summaries.population: 402 children from the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL)
limitations: Small sample size (402 children)
Focus on very low-level exposure
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary MaterialsClick here for additional data file.

summaries.title: Is intrauterine exposure to acetaminophen associated with emotional and hyperactivity problems during childhood? Findings from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort.
summaries.summary: A prospective longitudinal population-based study investigating the association between intrauterine acetaminophen exposure and emotional/hyperactivity problems in children at ages 6 and 11.
key_findings: Intrauterine acetaminophen exposure increased the odds of emotional problems (OR=1.47) and hyperactivity/inattention (OR=1.42) in 6-year-old boys.
No association was observed for girls at either age.
The effect size decreased slightly at age 11 after adjustment.
summaries.population: Children in the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort, Brazil
limitations: No association observed in girls
Small decrease in effect size at age 11

summaries.title: Quantitative characterization of the urine and serum metabolomes of children is essential for 'omics' studies.
summaries.summary: Commentary on a study by Lau et al. characterizing the serum and urine metabolomes of European children to establish a reference for future exposome studies.
key_findings: Metabolites were associated with BMI, diet, and demographics.
Novel association between urinary 4-deoxyerythronic acid and BMI.
Establishment of a reference metabolome for children to facilitate future 'omics' research.
summaries.population: European children
limitations: Lack of existing pediatric metabolomic references
Commentary format limits primary data analysis
summaries.data_availability_statement: Not applicable.

summaries.title: Prenatal Exposure to Select Phthalates and Phenols and Associations with Fetal and Placental Weight among Male Births in the EDEN Cohort (France).
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the associations between maternal prenatal exposure to phthalates and phenols and fetal/placental weight in male births.
key_findings: Triclosan and monocarboxy-isononyl phthalate (MCNP) were negatively associated with placental weight.
Benzophenone-3 and the sum of parabens were positively associated with placental weight.
MCNP and monocarboxy-isooctyl phthalate (MCOP) were negatively associated with the placental-to-birth weight ratio (PFR).
captured_features: 9 phenols (4 parabens, 2 dichlorophenols, triclosan, benzophenone-3, bisphenol A)
summaries.population: 473 mother-son pairs in the EDEN cohort (France)
limitations: preliminary evidence
hypothesis generating
need for repeated assessments of exposure

summaries.title: Development of a novel multiplex electrochemiluminescent-based immunoassay to aid enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli vaccine development and evaluations.
summaries.summary: Development and validation of a novel multiplex electrochemiluminescent (ECL) immunoassay using the Meso Scale Discovery (MSD) platform to analyze immune responses to Enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli (ETEC) antigens.
key_findings: The MSD platform achieved excellent correlations with ELISA for the antigens tested.
The 8-plex assay consistently detected comparable antibody levels.
A single dilution of sample provided quantifiable results for a wide range of sample titers.
captured_features: Antibody titers for CFA/I, CS1, CS2, CS3, CS5, CS6, LTA, and LTB
summaries.population: Not applicable (laboratory study)
limitations: Laboratory-based validation, not a clinical trial

summaries.title: Dietary Patterns Are Not Consistently Associated with Variability in Blood Lead Concentrations in Pregnant British Women.
summaries.summary: A study evaluating the association between dietary patterns (derived from food-frequency questionnaires) and blood lead concentrations (B-Pb) in pregnant women within the ALSPAC birth cohort.
key_findings: Negative association between 'confectionery' dietary pattern and B-Pb >= 5 ug/dL
Positive association between 'all leafy green and green vegetables' and B-Pb >= 5 ug/dL
Negative association between 'cakes and biscuits' and B-Pb >= 5 ug/dL
Limited evidence of consistent association between typical diet and B-Pb during pregnancy
summaries.population: pregnant women in Avon, UK
limitations: limited evidence of consistent association
reliance on food-frequency questionnaires
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary Materialsnxz023_Supplemental_FileClick here for additional data file.

summaries.title: Mitotically heritable effects of BMAA on striatal neural stem cell proliferation and differentiation.
summaries.summary: Experimental study investigating the effects of the cyanobacterial toxin BMAA on the proliferation, differentiation, and mitotically heritable traits of primary striatal neurons and embryonic neural stem cells.
key_findings: BMAA reduced neural stem cell proliferation via apoptosis and G2/M arrest at 250 µM.
At 50-100 µM, BMAA reduced differentiation into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons via glutamatergic mechanisms.
BMAA-induced changes were mitotically heritable to daughter cells.
BMAA-treated stem cells produced neurons with reduced neurite length and fewer processes.
summaries.population: primary striatal neurons and embryonic neural stem cells
limitations: in vitro model
not a human epidemiological study

summaries.title: The effect of H1N1 vaccination on serum miRNA expression in children: A tale of caution for microRNA microarray studies.
summaries.summary: A study investigating changes in serum microRNA (miRNA) expression in children 21 days after receiving an H1N1 vaccine using microarray and RT-qPCR.
key_findings: Microarray revealed 19 differentially expressed microRNAs, but many could not be validated by RT-qPCR.
RT-qPCR confirmed downregulation of miR-142-3p in the discovery cohort, but this was not replicated in the validation cohort.
Highlights the need for caution and validation when interpreting miRNA microarray results.
summaries.study_design: Open-label randomised control trial (discovery and validation cohorts)
summaries.population: Children aged 6 months to 12 years
limitations: Small sample size (n=22 in validation cohort)
Lack of replication in validation cohort

summaries.title: Repeated holdout validation for weighted quantile sum regression.
summaries.summary: The study proposes and validates a 'repeated holdout validation' method for Weighted Quantile Sum (WQS) regression to assess chemical mixtures' impacts on health outcomes, specifically testing it on prenatal exposure to endocrine disrupting chemicals and child IQ.
key_findings: Repeated holdout validation stabilizes WQS estimates and characterizes uncertainty in chemical weights.
Single partition results were unstable and varied by random seed.
WQS index estimate for prenatal exposure to endocrine disruptors was attenuated and non-significant when using repeated holdout validation compared to using all data.
captured_features: Prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals
summaries.study_design: Methodological validation study using a longitudinal cohort
summaries.population: 718 mother-child pairs in the Swedish Environmental Longitudinal, Mother and Child, Asthma and Allergy (SELMA) study
limitations: Small sample size (718 pairs) for complex mixture modeling
summaries.data_availability_statement: E.M. Tanner, C. Gennings, evamtanner/Repeated_Holdout_WQS: 1st Rodeo (Version v1.0.0). [WWW Document]. Zenodo., (2019), doi:http://dx.doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.2658697.

summaries.title: Serious adverse event: late neurotropic disease associated with yellow fever vaccine.
summaries.summary: A case report of a healthy infant who developed a late neurotropic disease following a yellow fever vaccine.
key_findings: The patient developed strabismus, cervical muscle weakness, and reduced leg strength 20 days after yellow fever vaccination.
Liquor analysis confirmed positive IgM for yellow fever, confirming vaccine-associated neurotropic disease.
summaries.population: infant
limitations: single case report
not an EWAS or large-scale cohort study

summaries.title: Early-life inhalant allergen exposure, filaggrin genotype, and the development of sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal birth cohort study investigating how filaggrin (FLG) mutations modify the relationship between early-life inhalant allergen exposure (mite, cat, dog) and allergic sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
key_findings: Significant interaction between FLG mutations and Fel d 1 exposure on cat sensitization (OR 1.36).
Risk of mite sensitization with increasing Der p 1 exposure was consistently greater in children with FLG mutations (non-significant interaction).
Significant interaction between FLG and dog ownership, where risk of sensitization was significantly lower among children with FLG mutations exposed to a dog in infancy (OR 0.16).
captured_features: Mite, cat, and dog allergen levels in dust samples
summaries.population: 1184 subjects in the Manchester Asthma and Allergy Study
summaries.exposure_domain: inhalant allergens (house dust mite, cat, dog)
limitations: none specified in text
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary MaterialsOnline Repository

summaries.title: BCG Moreau Vaccine Safety Profile and NK Cells-Double Protection Against Disseminated BCG Infection in Retrospective Study of BCG Vaccination in 52 Polish Children with Severe Combined Immunodeficiency.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The profiles of 52 SCID patients vaccinated at birth with BCG, hospitalized in Children ’s Memorial Health Institute, Warsaw (CMHI), in the years 1980–2015 were compared...
summaries.summary: A retrospective study comparing the safety profile of the BCG Moreau vaccine in Polish children with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) against international data.
key_findings: Significantly lower rate of disseminated BCG infections (19% vs 34% in the Marciano study)
NK cells play a role in protecting SCID patients against disseminated BCG complications
Higher number of hematopoietic stem cell transplantations (HSCT) in the CMHI study
BCG Moreau vaccine appears safer than other substrains used in other regions
summaries.population: 52 SCID patients in Poland (1980-2015)
limitations: Small sample size (n=52)
Retrospective design

summaries.title: Childhood type 1 diabetes: an environment-wide association study across England.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We obtained type 1 diabetes data from National Health Service (NHS) Digital hospital episode statistics (HES) records.
summaries.summary: An ecological environment-wide association study (EnW AS) investigating environmental and demographic risk factors associated with childhood type 1 diabetes incidence in England.
key_findings: 15 out of 53 factors were significantly associated with diabetes incidence
Air pollutants (PM10, NO2, NOx, CO) were inversely associated
Significant associations found with lead in soil, radon, outdoor light at night, overcrowding, population density, and ethnicity
Association found between type 1 diabetes and the living environment domain of the Index of Multiple Deprivation and radon potential class
captured_features: LAD level demographic and environmental risk factors
summaries.population: children aged 0-9 years in England
summaries.exposure_domain: environmental pollution, meteorological, land use, and demographic factors
limitations: ecological design
summaries.data_availability_statement: The majority of the demographic and environmental factors included in the study were derived from Crown copyright data and are available via the links provided in the text and references. HES data are available via NHS Digital on application with appropriate ethics and governance permissions; we do not hold data provider, ethics or governance permissions to share these health data with third parties.

summaries.title: Unilateral morning glory disc anomaly in a patient with prenatal Zika virus exposure.
summaries.summary: A case report describing the first instance of morning glory disc anomaly (MGDA) in a pediatric patient with confirmed prenatal Zika virus (ZIKV) exposure.
key_findings: Prenatal ZIKV exposure may be associated with a wider pathologic spectrum than previously reported, including MGDA.
The patient presented with unilateral MGDA, base of skull encephalocele, and callosal dysgenesis.
summaries.population: 3-year-old male child
limitations: single case report
no large-scale cohort data
summaries.data_availability_statement: All data generated or analyzed during this study are included in this manuscript.

summaries.title: Early-Life Famine Exposure and Risk of Cardiovascular Diseases in Later Life: Findings From the REACTION Study.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study investigating the association between early-life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959-1962) and the risk of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in adulthood.
key_findings: Early-life famine exposure significantly increased the risk of total CVD, myocardial infarction, stroke, and coronary heart disease.
Individuals in severely affected famine areas had a significantly higher risk of CVD compared to those in less severely affected areas.
Odds ratios for CVD were elevated across fetal, childhood, and adolescent famine exposure groups.
summaries.population: 259,657 community-dwelling adults aged 40 years or older from mainland China
limitations: Historical cohort limitations
Potential for recall bias in famine exposure
summaries.data_availability_statement:

summaries.title: Long-Term Effects of Environmental Lead on Erythropoietin Production in Young Adults: A Follow-Up Study of a Prospective Cohort in Kosovo.
summaries.summary: A prospective follow-up study evaluating the long-term effects of prenatal and childhood lead (Pb) exposure on erythropoietin (EPO) production in young adults.
key_findings: Participants exposed to high levels of environmental Pb in childhood showed a reduced ability to hyper-produce EPO to maintain normal hemoglobin levels in young adulthood.
The results suggest cumulative toxicity to the peritubular cells of the kidney responsible for EPO synthesis.
summaries.population: 101 participants (mean age 24.9 years) from a pediatric cohort in Kosovo
limitations: Challenges in recruiting participants from Prishtina due to city expansion and ethnic changes
Small sample size (n=101) for the follow-up
summaries.data_availability_statement: All pertinent data are in our study files and can be made available upon request.

summaries.title: Warp Speed for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) Vaccines: Why Are Children Stuck in Neutral?
summaries.summary: A viewpoint piece advocating for the immediate initiation of phase 2 clinical trials for COVID-19 vaccines in children to address safety, immunogenicity, and public health impacts.
key_findings: Pediatric COVID-19 hospitalizations rival pre-vaccine burdens of other viruses.
High rates of ICU admission for MIS-C and PCR-positive cases.
Significant racial disparities in COVID-19 severity among children.
summaries.population: Children in the United States
limitations: Not an original research study
Opinion/Viewpoint piece

summaries.title: DNA methylome signatures of prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids in hippocampus and peripheral whole blood of female guinea pigs in early life.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the DNA methylome signatures of prenatal synthetic glucocorticoid (sGC) exposure in the hippocampus and peripheral whole blood of juvenile female guinea pigs.
key_findings: Over 90% of the common methylation signatures associated with sGC exposure showed the same directionality of change.
134 genes were modified in both hippocampus and blood, with 61 showing changes at identical CpG sites.
Prenatal sGC exposure alters the methylation status of gene clusters involved in brain development.
summaries.population: Juvenile female guinea pigs
limitations: Animal model (guinea pigs) rather than human data
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data discussed in this publication have been deposited in NCBI ’s Gene Expression Omnibus 48 and are accessible through GEO Series accession number GSE163447.

summaries.title: Assessment of chronic bronchitis and risk factors in young adults: results from BAMSE.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used questionnaire data and clinical measures from the 24-year follow-up of the Swedish BAMSE (Child (Barn), Allergy, Milieu, Stockholm, Epidemiological) cohort.
summaries.summary: A study assessing the prevalence and early-life risk factors for chronic bronchitis in young adults using data from a Swedish population-based birth cohort.
key_findings: Prevalence of chronic bronchitis in young adults was 5.5%.
Daily smoking was associated with chronic bronchitis (aOR 3.85).
Air pollution exposure (black carbon at ages 1–4y) was associated with chronic bronchitis (aOR 1.71 per 1μg·m⁻³ increase).
Exclusive breastfeeding for ≥4 months was associated with lower risk of chronic bronchitis (aOR 0.66).
summaries.population: young adults in a Swedish population-based birth cohort
limitations: Not explicitly stated in the provided text snippet

summaries.title: Aspartame and cancer - new evidence for causation.
summaries.summary: Commentary on the re-evaluation of Ramazzini Institute studies regarding the carcinogenicity of aspartame in rodents.
key_findings: Immunohistochemical and morphological re-evaluation confirmed malignancy in 92.3% of cases.
Prenatal exposure to aspartame increases cancer risk in rodent offspring.
No evidence of Mycoplasma infection was found.
summaries.population: Sprague-Dawley rats and Swiss mice
limitations: Animal study (rodents), not human data

summaries.title: Immunogenicity and Safety of AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 Influenza Vaccine in Children 6-35 Months of Age: Results From a Phase 2, Randomized, Observer-blind, Multicenter, Dose-ranging Study.
summaries.summary: A phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study evaluating the immunogenicity and safety of different formulations of an AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 influenza vaccine in children aged 6–35 months.
key_findings: All formulations elicited strong homologous immune responses.
1.9 µg HA/AS03B ranked highest on the immunogenicity-fever index.
Antibody levels persisted >4 times above baseline 12 months after primary vaccination.
1.9 µg HA/AS03B provided the most favorable balance of immunogenicity versus reactogenicity.
summaries.study_design: Phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study
summaries.population: children 6–35 months of age
limitations: observer-blind design

summaries.title: Maternal high-fat diet modifies myelin organization, microglial interactions, and results in social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits in adolescent mouse offspring.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the effects of maternal high-fat diet (mHFD) on myelin organization, microglial interactions, and behavioral outcomes in adolescent mouse offspring.
key_findings: mHFD-exposed male offspring showed increased myelin density but reduced cytosolic myelin channel area in the corpus callosum.
mHFD-exposed offspring showed loss of social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits.
Microglia in the corpus callosum of mHFD-exposed offspring showed reduced mature lysosomes and increased synaptic contacts.
Lower transcripts of myelination-associated genes (e.g., Igf1) were found in the hippocampus of male offspring.
summaries.population: adolescent mouse offspring
limitations: animal model (mice)

summaries.title: The Rotavirus Vaccine Story: From Discovery to the Eventual Control of Rotavirus Disease.
summaries.summary: A historical overview of the discovery, development, and global impact of rotavirus vaccines, highlighting the transition from identifying rotavirus as a cause of childhood diarrhea to the licensing of multiple vaccines.
key_findings: Rotavirus is a leading cause of severe diarrhea and under-5 mortality.
The first vaccine (RotaShield) was withdrawn due to intussusception.
Current vaccines (Rotarix, Rotateq, Rotavac, Rotasiil) have significantly reduced hospitalizations and deaths.
Live oral vaccines are less effective in low-income countries for reasons not yet fully understood.
captured_features: vaccine types (RotaShield, Rotarix, Rotateq, Rotavac, Rotasiil)
summaries.population: Children under 5 years old
limitations: Historical review, not a primary EWAS or EHR-based study

key_findings: Maternal stressful life events in late gestation were associated with greater uterine volume and higher ovarian antral follicle count (AFC) in offspring aged 14-16 years.

summaries.title: COVID-19 Vaccination Responses with Different Vaccine Platforms in Patients with Inborn Errors of Immunity.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of the safety and immunogenicity of various COVID-19 vaccine platforms (Sputnik, AstraZeneca, Sinopharm, Moderna, and Pfizer) in patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) in Argentina.
key_findings: No moderate/severe vaccine-associated adverse events observed.
Anti-S/RBD titers and neutralizing antibodies were significantly lower in IEI patients compared to healthy controls.
Moderna vaccines elicited a stronger humoral response in IEI patients compared to other platforms.
Positive S-specific IFN-γ response observed in 84.5% of IEI patients.
Cellular immune response was similar across different vaccine platforms.
summaries.population: 118 adults and pediatric IEI patients and 37 age-matched healthy controls in Argentina
limitations: most vaccine data comes from trials in healthy individuals
summaries.data_availability_statement: All data generated or analyzed during this study are included in this published article (and its supplementary information files).

summaries.title: Open-label phase I/II clinical trial of SARS-CoV-2 receptor binding domain-tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine (FINLAY-FR-2) in combination with receptor binding domain-protein vaccine (FINLAY-FR-1A) in children.
summaries.summary: A phase I/II open-label clinical trial evaluating the safety and immunogenicity of a heterologous SARS-CoV-2 vaccine scheme (SOBERANA 02 and SOBERANA Plus) in children.
key_findings: Local pain was the only adverse event > 10%
Two doses of FINLAY-FR-2 elicited a humoral immune response similar to natural infection
The third heterologous dose with FINLAY-FR-1A increased the response in all children
GMT neutralizing titers were measured against Alpha, Delta, Beta, and Omicron variants
summaries.population: 350 children 3-18 years old in Havana, Cuba
limitations: Small sample size (350 children)
Phase I/II trial

summaries.title: Associations of longitudinal BMI percentile classification patterns in early childhood with neighborhood-level social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We extracted EHR data from 2012-2019 for a children’s health system that includes 2 hospitals and wide network of outpatient clinics spanning 5 East Coast states in the US.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study identifying distinct BMI-percentile classification groups in children aged 0-7 and examining their associations with neighborhood-level social determinants of health (SDOH).
key_findings: Five BMI-percentile classification groups emerged: always having obesity, overweight most of the time, increasing BMI-percentile, decreasing BMI-percentile, and always normal weight.

Children in the obesity and increasing BMI groups were more likely to live in neighborhoods with higher poverty, unemployment, crowded households, single-parent households, and lower preschool enrollment.
summaries.study_design: longitudinal cohort study using algorithmic clustering and multinomial logistic regression
summaries.population: 36,910 children aged 0 to 7 years in 5 East Coast states, US
limitations: not certified by peer review (preprint)

summaries.title: Correction: Neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium term lead-related outcomes in children ≤5 years old with congenital lead poisoning: A retrospective cohort study in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.summary: Correction notice for a retrospective cohort study investigating how neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium-term lead-related outcomes in children under 5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria.
key_findings: Neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium-term lead-related outcomes in children <5 years old with congenital lead poisoning.
summaries.population: children <5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria
limitations: Correction notice only; full methodology not provided in this text

summaries.title: Activated fibroblasts modify keratinocyte stem niche through TET1 and IL-6 to promote their rapid transformation in a mouse model of prenatal arsenic exposure.
summaries.summary: A study investigating how prenatal arsenic exposure leads to skin tumorigenesis in mouse offspring by examining the role of arsenic-conditioned dermal fibroblasts in modifying the keratinocyte stem cell niche.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure induces activation markers (α-SMA, Fibronectin, Collagen IV) in dermal fibroblasts.
Arsenic-conditioned fibroblasts show increased TET1 expression and IL-6 secretion.
IL-6 promotes proliferation, migration, and survival of keratinocyte stem cells via JAK2-STAT3 signaling.
Inhibition of pSTAT3 reduces EMT and proliferation in keratinocyte stem cells.
summaries.study_design: In vivo mouse model and in vitro mechanistic study
summaries.population: BALB/c mouse offspring
limitations: Animal model (mouse)
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets generated and analyzed during the current study are submitted to CSIR-IITR repository and will be made available by corresponding author on request.

summaries.title: Genetic exploration of Dravet syndrome: two case report.
summaries.summary: A case series of two Indonesian children with Dravet syndrome (DS) focusing on genetic analysis using next-generation sequencing (NGS) to identify SCN1A mutations and other associated genes.
key_findings: Patient 1 had heterozygous deletion mutations in TTC21B, SCN1A, and SCN9A.
Patient 2 had a previously unreported mutation at the canonical splice site upstream of exon 24 of the SCN1A gene.
Clinical severity correlated with the severity of the genetic mutation.
summaries.population: two Indonesian children (aged 11 years 2 months and 1 year 4 months)
limitations: small sample size (n=2)
not an exposome/environmental study

summaries.title: Comparison of 1 Versus 2 Doses of Quadrivalent Influenza Vaccine in 3-8-Year-Old Children with Different Immunological States - Jiangsu Province, China, 2021.
summaries.summary: A clinical trial comparing the immunogenicity and safety of one versus two doses of quadrivalent influenza vaccine (QIV) in children aged 3–8 years in China, stratified by previous vaccination history.
key_findings: Evidence supports a single dose of QIV for children aged 3–8 years who have previously received two or more doses of influenza vaccine.
Children with no prior vaccination should still receive two doses.
summaries.population: healthy children aged 3–8 years in Jiangsu Province, China
limitations: Not specified in text
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary data to this article can be found online.

summaries.ehr_evidence: None None None None None None None None
summaries.summary: A study investigating the impact of prenatal betamethasone (BM) exposure on cognitive performance (IQ), ADHD symptoms, BMI, and insulin resistance in children.
key_findings: No statistically significant differences in IQ scores between BM and reference groups after adjusting for SES and sex.
No dose-response relationship identified between BM exposure and cognitive performance.
No statistically significant differences in core ADHD symptoms.
No significant differences in BMI or HOMA-IR.
No statistically significant difference in IQ between BM and reference groups.

No significant differences in HPAA or most ANS parameters after adjusting for covariates.

Significant differences were found in saliva alpha-amylase levels between groups.
BM exposure associated with maturational changes in cortical/subcortical brain structures (SEF changes).
No statistically significant association between intrauterine BM and insulin resistance or BMI.
HPAA activity during TSST was not associated with prenatal BM exposure.

summaries.title: Newborn glomerular function and gestational particulate air pollution.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the ENVIRONAGE cohort, which includes mothers giving birth at a hospital and participants filling out a Dutch questionnaire.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the impact of gestational particulate air pollution (black carbon and PM2.5) on cord blood cystatin C levels, a marker for glomerular function in newborns.
key_findings: Every 0.5 μg/m3 and 5 μg/m3 increment in gestational exposure to black carbon (BC) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) corresponded to increases of 0.04 mg/L and 0.07 mg/L in cord blood cystatin C levels, respectively.
Third-trimester exposure showed similar associations.
captured_features: gestational exposure to fine particulate matter (PM2.5)
summaries.population: 1484 mother-child pairs in the ENVIRONAGE cohort (East-Limburg hospital, Belgium)
limitations: not specified

summaries.title: Associations between gestational exposure to perfluoroalkyl substances, fetal growth, and the mediation effect of thyroid hormones.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the associations between maternal serum PFAS concentrations and neonatal growth metrics, specifically examining the mediating role of thyroid hormones.
key_findings: PFNA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth length.
PFOA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth head circumference.
Maternal FT3 mediated 36.7% of the negative association between PFNA and birth length.
Maternal FT4 mediated 30.8% of the effect of PFOA on head circumference.
summaries.population: 374 mother-infant pairs from the Ezhou birth cohort study (2019-2020)
limitations: Small sample size (374 pairs)
Potential for sex-based differences not fully explored
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets used and/or analysed during the current study available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.

summaries.title: Early Life Exposure to Manure-Fertilized Soil Shapes the Gut Antibiotic Resistome.
summaries.summary: A study investigating how early life exposure to pig manure-fertilized soil (red and black soils) affects the gut antibiotic resistome in mice.
key_findings: Manure fertilization significantly enriched tetracycline-resistant ARGs in both soil types.
Mice in these environments had higher ARG abundances, specifically the tet(Q) gene.
Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae were identified as key hosts for tet(Q) with evidence of horizontal gene transfer.
summaries.population: Mice (raised from birth to 8 weeks)
limitations: Animal model (mice) rather than human data
summaries.data_availability_statement: The raw metagenomic sequence data are publicly available under the accession number PRJNA1206751.

summaries.title: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors.
summaries.summary: A comprehensive atlas of the gut bacteriome of 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus), analyzing the influence of host and exposome factors on microbial diversity.
key_findings: Clear differences in gut bacterial diversity between wild and captive bonobos.
Factors such as age, transit time, diet, early life adversity, and medication use influence gut bacterial structure in zoo-housed cohorts.
Social contact is a stronger predictor of bacteriome similarity than genetics or other exposome factors.
Maternal effects persist even in non-cohabiting mother-offspring dyads.
summaries.population: 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus)
summaries.exposure_domain: exposome (diet, medication, social contact, early life adversity, industrialization)
limitations: Not applicable (primary data collection)

summaries.title: Using machine learning to predict future foster care admission.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We use claims data for children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio as well as for linked adults.
summaries.summary: A study using machine learning to predict future foster care admissions for children in Ohio using Medicaid claims and social determinants of health (SDOH) data.
key_findings: Gradient-boosted tree model identified 1.32% of children at risk of foster care admission.
Positive predictive value (PPV) was 66.4% (F1 = 55.5%, specificity = 99.5%, sensitivity = 47.67%).
Accuracy was substantially better with SDOH data (PPV of 84.72%) compared to without (PPV of 27.44%).
captured_features: individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH)
summaries.population: children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio
limitations: ethical and legal concerns regarding use for selecting families for investigations
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary material is available at Health Affairs Scholar online.

summaries.title: Moderate prenatal alcohol exposure differentially alters acute ethanol sensitivity of GABAergic transmission in CRFR1- and CRFR1+ CeM Neurons.
summaries.summary: The study investigates how moderate prenatal alcohol exposure (PAE) alters the acute effects of ethanol on GABAergic neurotransmission in the medial nucleus of the central amygdala (CeM) of adult rats.
key_findings: PAE alters acute EtOH modulation of GABA transmission in a cell type- and concentration-dependent manner.
Effects of PAE on GABAergic transmission in the CeM are sex-specific.
PAE-induced neuroadaptations persist into adulthood.
summaries.population: Adult male and female rats (P80-105)
limitations: Animal model study (rats)

summaries.title: Sugar restriction in the first 1000 days after conception, and long-term respiratory health: a quasi-experiment study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Outcomes included incident asthma, COPD, and IPF (ascertained by health records), validated in the English Longitudinal Study of Ageing (a United Kingdom cohort) and the Health and Retirement Study (a United States cohort).
summaries.summary: A quasi-experimental study investigating the long-term effects of early-life sugar restriction (during the first 1000 days after conception) on adult respiratory health, specifically asthma, COPD, and IPF.
key_findings: Early-life sugar rationing was associated with lower risks of asthma (HR: 0.75) and COPD (HR: 0.73).
Sugar restriction was associated with delayed disease onset by ≤ 3.6 years.
Rationed participants had significantly higher mean FEV1%, FVC%, and FEV1/FVC ratios.
Diabetes and hypertension mediated 18.2% of the effect.
summaries.data_source_type: EHR / Linked health data (UK Biobank, ELSA, HRS)
summaries.population: 58,670 UK Biobank participants born 1951–1956
limitations: quasi-experimental design
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data are accessible through a public, open-access repository . Data from the UKB, ELSA, and HRS are available to researchers on request.

summaries.title: Impact of maternal fasting blood glucose in pregnancy on body composition, anthropometric, and metabolic outcomes in newborns and 3-Year-Old offspring: results from the lifestyle in pregnancy and offspring study.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the impact of maternal fasting plasma glucose during pregnancy on offspring body composition, anthropometry, and metabolic outcomes at birth and at 3 years of age.
key_findings: Higher maternal fasting glucose was associated with a higher birth weight z-score.
Glucose >= 5.6 mmol/L predicted higher birth weight.
No significant associations were found for BMI z-score or metabolic markers at 3 years.
summaries.population: Children born to 301 pregnant women in Denmark
limitations: Small sample size for 3-year follow-up (157 participants)
Small number of children with DXA assessments (75)
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data that support the findings of this study are available from C.A.V., but restrictions apply to their availability. The data were used under license for the current study and include unpublished results that are still being analyzed. Therefore, they are not publicly available. However, data may be obtained from the corresponding author on reasonable request and with permission from C.A.V.

summaries.title: Maternal exposure to cyclophosphamide during prepubertal life does not affect the cryosusceptibility of in vitro derived mouse embryos.
summaries.summary: A mouse model study investigating whether prepubertal exposure to the chemotherapy drug cyclophosphamide (CY) affects the cryosusceptibility (survival and quality) of embryos derived via in vitro fertilization (IVF).
key_findings: No significant differences were found between CY-exposed and control groups in post-warming embryo survival.
No significant differences in the expression of pluripotency markers (Oct4, Sox2, Nanog) were observed.
summaries.population: Mice (female)
limitations: Animal model study (not human data)
